scenario_description: User asks to sort by a column, but the instruction is ambiguous. After agent sorts by the wrong column, user clarifies the correct one.

command: DISPLAY=:1 gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.screensaver idle-activation-enabled false 2>/dev/null; DISPLAY=:1 gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.session idle-delay 0 2>/dev/null; DISPLAY=:1 xset s off 2>/dev/null; DISPLAY=:1 xset -dpms 2>/dev/null; true
DISPLAY=:1 xdotool key Escape 2>/dev/null; sleep 0.3; DISPLAY=:1 xdotool search --class 'soffice' windowfocus windowraise 2>/dev/null || DISPLAY=:1 wmctrl -xa 'soffice.Soffice' 2>/dev/null || true

phases.instruction: Sort the data by the highest numbers
No, that's the wrong column! I meant sort by the March column specifically, from highest to lowest. Please undo what you did and redo it correctly.
Good, now also add a Total row at the bottom with SUM for each column, and save.

result.command: python3 -c "import pandas as pd; df=pd.read_excel('/home/user/Desktop/SalesRep.xlsx'); c=df.iloc[:,0].astype(str).str.lower(); print('PASSED' if c.str.contains('total').any() else 'FAILED')"
